author: potuz

author: potuz

author: nflaig
content: the 2nd comment is the same as https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5094#discussion_r3075067272 ?

author: potuz
content: Ah lol yes I remembered sending that lol. Could you please mark as resolved the last one? As for the test, I think it's fine to rely on deserialization for the Ssz limits but not for the per payload limits. This requires the engine to be correct and in sync with the CL when we can just quickly catch these in the CL statically

author: nflaig
content: mark as resolved and linked to the other comment, what do you mean by payload limits?

author: nflaig
content: do we wanna keep `[REJECT] hash_tree_root(envelope.execution_requests) == bid.execution_requests_root` in gossip validation? it's already check in `verify_execution_payload_envelope` so technically not needed, not sure if there is a risk to removing it from gossip

author: potuz
content: check that the length of the requests is below the maximum allowed per limit

author: potuz
content: consensus objects should be checked on consensus, regardless if the EL makes the same checks

author: nflaig
content: ok but consensus checks it via ssz limits already

author: nflaig
content: I was talking to Justin about something related to this, if the gas limit is above 194M then you could stuff the payload with enough deposits to get above 8192 which is the ssz limit on the CL side so the block would be invalid

author: potuz
content: No, the Ssz limits are typically not the enforced limits, are they the same in this case? Even if they were I think we want to check them cause these numbers are independent

author: nflaig
content: but for example you have `deposits: List[DepositRequest, MAX_DEPOSIT_REQUESTS_PER_PAYLOAD]` and so the list limit is `8192` if your receive `execution_request.deposit.lenght > 8192` then deserialization fails at gossip layer

author: potuz
content: Yes so in this case the enforced limit is the Ssz limits. We should still check it IMO. Most Ssz limits are different and nothing prevents is from changing this in the future. Adding an assert in the spec is innocuous and clients may completely ignore it cause it's the same today
